name: Import crossasset model from wolf
description: Wolf imports crossasset as a first-party package instead of reaching into zerfoo/crossasset.
preconditions: crossasset package exists in wolf repo
expected_outcome: Wolf's train-crossasset CLI and internal/model/crossasset.go import from wolf's own crossasset package.
note: Completed via E91 (2026-04-13, ADR-084). Retained for history.

name: Train crossasset model via wolf CLI
description: Wolf's train-crossasset command trains a crossasset model using the extracted package.
expected_outcome: Training completes, model saved, loss decreases.
note: Completed via E91 (2026-04-13, ADR-084). Retained for history.

name: Zerfoo builds without crossasset
description: After extraction, zerfoo compiles and all non-crossasset tests pass.
expected_outcome: go build ./... and go test ./... pass with no crossasset references.
note: Completed via E91 (2026-04-13, ADR-084). Retained for history.

name: Capture-replay training fails loudly when unsafe
description: A user who enables CUDA-graph capture-replay training on a version where #878 (silent gradient divergence) is unfixed gets an explicit error or documented hard gate at Trainer construction, never silently-wrong gradients.
expected_outcome: Capture-replay training refuses to run (clear error naming #878 and the opt-out env var) until the divergence bug is fixed; eager training is unaffected.

test_data.model: small synthetic graph, [ns,3] logits shape from #878 repro

note: Completed in Phase 0 (2026-07-02): UC-H2-001 via PR #920 gate (v1.56.0); UC-H2-002 via PR #915 + hardening (see devlog).

name: One-command DGX arm64 validation run
description: A developer (or CI trigger) runs a single script that builds zerfoo natively on the GB10 (arm64, real CUDA) via a Spark pod and executes the GPU test + parity suite, producing a pass/fail report. Eliminates the purego darwin-to-linux cross-compile blocker class permanently.
expected_outcome: Pod builds natively on the DGX, runs the suite, exits with pass/fail and a JSON report; results recorded in devlog. Closes the T86.5.8 class of stalled validation tasks.

test_data.suite: tests/parity GPU subset + -tags cuda unit tests

note: Completed in Phase 0 (2026-07-02): UC-H2-001 via PR #920 gate (v1.56.0); UC-H2-002 via PR #915 + hardening (see devlog).

name: Capture-replay training trains correctly
description: With CUDA-graph capture-replay enabled, training converges identically to eager mode; the #878 fixture is green on GB10 and the containment gate is removed.

name: Verified-model matrix published with evidence
description: docs/verified-models.md lists ~10 flagship models each with parity + benchmark + GPU evidence; marketing claims may not exceed it; Ollama comparison reproducible via manifests.
preconditions: models provisioned on DGX host (T136.2, human)
expected_outcome: Matrix live; benchmarks.md refreshed; T86.5.8 closed.

name: gemma4e decode correct or honestly demoted
description: The #766 native-Q4_K fix is executed once; decode verified on GB10, or gemma4 edge is marked experimental everywhere.
expected_outcome: #757/#766 closed either way; no public Gemma 4 claims unless fixed.

name: darwin dev hosts can run the test suite
description: ztensor device.init no longer SIGSEGVs on darwin; GPU-importing test packages skip cleanly on macs.
expected_outcome: go test ./tests/training/ skips (not crashes) on darwin; ztensor#171 closed.

name: Untrusted GGUF model files cannot crash the loader
description: A malformed or crafted GGUF file (integer-overflow dims, out-of-range offsets, oversized dim count) is rejected with a returned error, never a panic, on both the default mmap load path and split-file variants.
preconditions: A GGUF file is being loaded (local, pulled, or split)
expected_outcome: F1 (element-count overflow), F2 (offset signed-conversion), F3 (unbounded dim count) are all bounds-checked before use; a malformed-input fuzz corpus proves rejection with an error, not a panic.
note: deep-review 002 findings F1/F2/F3 + OCI-1/OCI-2 (digest verification, https-only); see docs/deep-reviews/002-full-codebase.md and plan.md E139

name: Distributed training wire is authenticated and encrypted
description: The distributed worker/coordinator gRPC surface refuses a non-loopback bind without mTLS, wires the existing tlsconfig.go credentials, and the coordinator authenticates worker registration before disclosing the peer list.
preconditions: distributed training is enabled (worker/coordinator processes running)
expected_outcome: DIST-1 (unauth/unencrypted worker bound to 0.0.0.0) and DIST-2 (coordinator peer-list disclosure, unauthenticated RegisterWorker) are closed; a routable bind requires TLS certs; loopback-only runs are unaffected.
note: deep-review 002 findings DIST-1/DIST-2; see plan.md E140

name: Native library loading only trusts vetted absolute paths
description: CUDA/HIP/OpenCL kernel and vendor libraries are never dlopen'd from the current working directory or by bare soname resolution that LD_LIBRARY_PATH can hijack; only vetted absolute paths are used.
preconditions: a zerfoo process initializes a GPU backend
expected_outcome: CUDA-1 (./libkernels.so CWD dlopen) and CUDA-2 (bare-soname LD_LIBRARY_PATH hijack) are closed; kernelLibPaths contains only absolute, vetted paths.
note: deep-review 002 findings CUDA-1/CUDA-2; see plan.md E141

name: Concurrent request handling has no data races or TOCTOU windows
description: Speculative decode is serialized through the same graph mutex as normal generation; model-delete cannot race a use-after-close; the rate limiter and API-key revocation state are race-free under -race.
preconditions: the HTTP server is handling concurrent requests
expected_outcome: CONC-H1 (SpeculativeGenerate lock bypass), CONC-H2 (model-delete TOCTOU/WaitGroup misuse), CONC-M1 (rate-limiter unbounded map), CONC-M2 (KeyStore field race), CONC-L1 (batch goroutine leak) are all closed; -race is clean on the affected packages.
note: deep-review 002 findings CONC-H1/H2/M1/M2/L1; see plan.md E142

name: HTTP API resource consumption and traversal are bounded
description: Adapter names cannot traverse outside the adapter directory; error-metric labels cannot grow unbounded from attacker-chosen paths; embeddings/image-fetch/guardian-body inputs are capped; sampling parameters are validated; SSRF blocklist covers unspecified/CGNAT ranges.
preconditions: the OpenAI-compatible HTTP server is running
expected_outcome: SERVE-1/2/3/3b/4/5/6/7, SSRF-1, HF-1 are closed with API tests asserting the real request/response boundary.
note: deep-review 002 findings SERVE-1/2/3/3b/4/5/6/7, SSRF-1, HF-1; see plan.md E143

name: CI/CD pipeline and container supply chain are hardened
description: GitHub Actions workflows declare least-privilege permissions blocks, pin mutable tool/package installs, scope PR-comment write tokens, digest-pin container base images, and track the outstanding bbolt advisory instead of silently continue-on-error.
preconditions: a PR or push triggers CI
expected_outcome: CICD-1 through CICD-6 are closed; SLSA provenance (cosign signing + SBOM) added as a stretch item.
note: deep-review 002 findings CICD-1..6, L1-L3, SLSA; see plan.md E144
